fields.title: Unify tau execution control around Effect + Schema execution profiles
fields.description: Move tau-managed execution behavior onto a single typed control-plane model shared by main sessions, Ralph loops, autoresearch segments, and spawned agents. Current state is split across prompt mode state, Ralph promptProfile persistence, agent mode resolution, and autoresearch ambient session behavior. The goal is for tau to own desired execution state in native Effect services with Schema-backed persistence while Pi remains the runtime kernel for sessions, UI, tool execution, auth, and model inventory.
fields.design: Introduce a first-class execution layer with: (1) prompt selector, (2) resolved prompt profile, (3) execution policy for tools/spawns, and (4) execution profile = prompt profile + policy. Add new execution schema/state/resolver/runtime services. Rewrite TauPersistedState around an execution subtree with legacy promptModes read-compat only. Keep /mode as the user-facing command, but make execution state canonical in tau rather than Pi getters. Ralph, autoresearch, and spawned agents should pin or resolve execution profiles through this shared contract. Tool policy should support inherit, require, and allowlist so main-session modes stay ergonomic while agents/loops can be strict. /agents should stay focused on enable/disable, not profile assignment UX.
